name: Click targeting
summary: Select an entity by pointer input and expose the selected target to combat or interaction systems.
problem_solved: Turns ambiguous player intent into a single game entity without coupling selection to combat rules.

source.license: local-owner workspace; do not redistribute without owner confirmation

engine_fits.reason: Source primitive came from a Godot learning project; still requires a seed engine ADR before adoption.
The interface is input-to-entity selection and can be reimplemented without Godot APIs.

verification_hooks.deterministic_test: given fixed entities and pointer positions, selection resolves to the same entity id
verification_hooks.bot_hook: bot can choose target ids through the same selection interface
verification_hooks.smoke_command: run the owning slice test suite; no standalone command until adopted

adoption_guard: Only adopt after a seed thesis needs explicit entity selection and the engine ADR earns a matching input surface.
slice_acceptance: A bot can select a target through the same interface as a player.
Selection has a deterministic headless test independent of rendering.
explicit_non_adoption: Do not copy Godot scene files or learning-workspace code into a child repo by default.
risks: Selection can become UI polish instead of core-loop proof if adopted before a slice needs it.
